channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-19698] TCU: .Trash dir can't be found in "uid" type
item.description:
This issue is very easy to reproduce by the following steps:

lctl set_param mdd.*.trash_can_enable=1
lctl set_param mdd.lustre-*.trash_can_type=uid
mkdir $mnt/d
chmod 777 $mnt/d
su $user1
touch $mnt/d/f_user1
rm $mnt/d/f_user1
exit
ls $mnt/d/.Trash

No .Trash directory is created under $mnt/d, but file f_user1 can be found under $mnt/.lustre/trash.
item.summary: TCU: .Trash dir can't be found in "uid" type

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.

comments.comment:
"Qian Yingjin <qian@ddn.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/63229
Subject: LU-19698 tcu: parent UID naming for UID Trash Can with nodemap
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 7e75b7f46b1e44bc4cddbdcedb39e7b81983e9af

"Qian Yingjin <qian@ddn.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/63228
Subject: LU-19698 tcu: add option to use parent UID to name in Trash Can
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 8d4a4d30780f94869922bf957fbe26e478a2a625

I wrote a test script to reproduce this problem:

test_118j() {
        (( MDS1_VERSION >= $(version_code 2.16.59) )) ||
                skip "Need MDS >= 2.16.59 for Trash client cache fix"
        [[ "$mds1_FSTYPE" == "ldiskfs" ]] || skip_env "ldiskfs only test"


        local dir=$DIR/$tdir
        local file=$dir/$tfile


        trash_setup "uid"


        mkdir_on_mdt0 $dir || error "failed to mkdir $dir"
        chmod 777 $dir || error "failed to chmod 777 $dir"
        stat $dir
        $RUNAS touch $file || error "failed to touch $file"
        ls -l $file
        $RUNAS unlink $file || error "failed to unlink $file"
        ls $dir/.Trash
        echo "Trash files under $MOUNT/.lustre/trash/MDT0000"
        ls -R $MOUNT/.lustre/trash/MDT0000
        tree $MOUNT/.lustre/trash
}
run_test 118j "TCU: access .Trash from non-root user"

The output:

mdd.lustre-MDT0000.trash_can_enable=1
mdd.lustre-MDT0000.trash_can_type=uid
Trash Can Type:
uid
  File: /mnt/lustre/d118j.sanityn
  Size: 4096      	Blocks: 8          IO Block: 1048576 directory
Device: 2c54f966h/743766374d	Inode: 144115205272502275  Links: 2
Access: (0777/drwxrwxrwx)  Uid: (    0/    root)   Gid: (    0/    root)
Access: 2025-12-26 16:55:24.000000000 +0800
Modify: 2025-12-26 16:55:24.000000000 +0800
Change: 2025-12-26 16:55:24.000000000 +0800
 Birth: 2025-12-26 16:55:24.000000000 +0800
running as uid/gid/euid/egid 500/500/500/500, groups: 500
 [touch] [/mnt/lustre/d118j.sanityn/f118j.sanityn]
-rw-r--r-- 1 test test 0 Dec 26 16:55 /mnt/lustre/d118j.sanityn/f118j.sanityn
running as uid/gid/euid/egid 500/500/500/500, groups: 500
 [unlink] [/mnt/lustre/d118j.sanityn/f118j.sanityn]
ls: cannot access '/mnt/lustre/d118j.sanityn/.Trash': No such file or directory
Trash files under /mnt/lustre/.lustre/trash/MDT0000
/mnt/lustre/.lustre/trash/MDT0000:
500


/mnt/lustre/.lustre/trash/MDT0000/500:
0x200000401:0x3:0x0


/mnt/lustre/.lustre/trash/MDT0000/500/0x200000401:0x3:0x0:
f118j.sanityn
/mnt/lustre/.lustre/trash
`-- MDT0000
    `-- 500
        `-- 0x200000401:0x3:0x0
            `-- f118j.sanityn


3 directories, 1 file
mdd.lustre-MDT0000.trash_can_enable=0
mdd.lustre-MDT0000.trash_can_type=uid

I do not think this is a bug.

The reason is that the UID of the parent dir (0) is different from the UID of files (500) under this directory. 

Once the files with UID=500 are removed from the normal Lustre namesake, we can not identify its UID from the parent UID (0).

There is no any easy way to solve this kind problem in TCU. The user itself needs to avoid this kind problem.

The possible solution maybe use the UID of the parent dir to identify pFID, but it may be hard to locate the pFID with UID of files or directories under the parent dir.

/mnt/lustre/.lustre/trash/MDT0000/500/0x200000401:0x3:0x0:
f118j.sanityn
/mnt/lustre/.lustre/trash
`-- MDT0000
    `-- 0
        `-- 0x200000401:0x3:0x0
            `-- f118j.sanityn

/mnt/lustre/tdir/dir1/f
dentry    UID
tdir         0
dir1        500
f             500

unlink f
/mnt/lustre/.lustre/trash
`-- MDT0000
    `-- 500
        `-- pFID(dir1)
            `-- f118j.sanityn

rmdir dir1
/mnt/lustre/.lustre/trash
`-- MDT0000
    `-- 0
        `-- pFID(tdir)
            `-- dir1
                `-- f

rename pFID(dir1) to "dir1" under pFID(tdir)

This solution can keep Trash Can namespace better, but it is broken the design principle that all files under UID are with its corresponding UID as it changes to use its parent UID to name in Trash Can for pFID which may be a different UID from its own UID.
